title: Spectral Token Gating: Training-Free Corruption Robustness for Vision Transformers via Frequency-Domain Token Analysis
description: We propose Spectral Token Gating (STG), a training-free, plug-and-play method that improves the corruption robustness of pretrained Vision Transformers by analyzing the frequency spectrum of patch token embeddings at inference time. STG detects tokens with anomalous spectral profiles (indicative of corruption) and gates their contribution to self-attention, effectively suppressing corrupted information without any retraining or parameter updates.
motivation: Vision Transformers suffer significant accuracy drops under common image corruptions (noise, blur, weather, digital) due to self-attention sensitivity to corrupted patches. Existing robustness methods require expensive retraining (RSPC, FAN, AugMax) or unstable test-time parameter updates (Tent). There is no training-free, plug-and-play approach that can improve corruption robustness of arbitrary pretrained ViTs. We observe that corruptions create detectable frequency-domain signatures in token embeddings, providing a lightweight signal for corruption detection at the token level.
proposed_approach: STG operates in four steps: (1) Offline calibration: collect spectral energy ratio statistics (mean, covariance) of token embeddings across frequency bands from a small set of clean images. (2) Test-time spectral analysis: compute the frequency spectrum of each token embedding and measure its Mahalanobis distance from the clean reference. (3) Gating: convert anomaly distances to soft gating scores via a sigmoid function. (4) Attention modulation: multiply value vectors by gating scores in self-attention, suppressing corrupted tokens while preserving clean ones. The method adds <2% computational overhead and requires no gradient computation.

related_work.title: Understanding The Robustness in Vision Transformers
related_work.authors: Daquan Zhou, Zhiding Yu, Enze Xie, Chaowei Xiao, Anima Anandkumar, Jiashi Feng, Jose M. Alvarez
related_work.relation: Proposes FAN architecture with channel self-attention for robustness. Requires training from scratch. Our method is training-free and works with any pretrained ViT.

related_work.title: Improving Robustness of Vision Transformers by Reducing Sensitivity to Patch Corruptions
related_work.authors: Yong Guo, David Stutz, Bernt Schiele
related_work.relation: Identifies patch-level corruption sensitivity and proposes adversarial training alignment. Requires retraining. Our method detects corrupted patches at test time via spectral analysis.

related_work.title: Robustifying Token Attention for Vision Transformers
related_work.authors: Yong Guo, David Stutz, Bernt Schiele
related_work.relation: Addresses token overfocusing with TAP and ADL, requiring retraining. Our method addresses the same problem (attention instability under corruption) but at test time via frequency-domain gating.

related_work.title: Tent: Fully Test-Time Adaptation by Entropy Minimization
related_work.authors: Dequan Wang, Evan Shelhamer, Shaoteng Liu, Bruno Olshausen, Trevor Darrell
related_work.relation: Test-time adaptation via entropy minimization on BN parameters. Requires parameter updates and careful batch handling. Our method requires no parameter updates, using statistics-based spectral gating instead.

related_work.title: Exploring Adversarial Robustness of Vision Transformers in the Spectral Perspective
related_work.relation: Analyzes ViT spectral vulnerabilities (reliance on low-freq/phase). Purely analytical with no defense proposed. We build on this analysis to design a practical defense via spectral token gating.

related_work.title: Token Merging: Your ViT But Faster
related_work.authors: Daniel Bolya, Cheng-Yang Fu, Xiaoliang Dai, Peizhao Zhang, Christoph Feichtenhofer, Judy Hoffman
related_work.relation: Training-free token reduction for efficiency. Focuses on merging redundant (not corrupted) tokens for speed. We adapt the paradigm of selective token processing for robustness rather than efficiency.

related_work.title: Benchmarking Neural Network Robustness to Common Corruptions and Perturbations
related_work.relation: Introduces the ImageNet-C and ImageNet-P benchmarks used for our evaluation.

related_work.title: SpectFormer: Frequency and Attention is What You Need in a Vision Transformer
related_work.authors: Badri N. Patro, Vijay S. Agneeswaran, Vinay P. Namboodiri
related_work.relation: Combines spectral layers with attention for classification performance. Architectural change requiring training. We use frequency analysis of token embeddings as a corruption signal at test time.

related_work.title: AugMax: Adversarial Composition of Random Augmentations for Robust Training
related_work.authors: Haotao Wang, Chaowei Xiao, Jean Kossaifi, Zhiding Yu, Animashree Anandkumar, Zhangyang Wang
related_work.relation: Training-time augmentation for robustness. Requires retraining with specialized augmentation. Our method is complementary and can be applied on top of AugMax-trained models.

hypothesis: Image corruptions create detectable frequency-domain anomalies in ViT patch token embeddings. By gating tokens with anomalous spectral profiles during self-attention computation, we can improve corruption robustness (reduce mCE by 3-8% on ImageNet-C) without any retraining or parameter updates, while maintaining clean accuracy within 0.5% of the original model.

primary: STG improves mCE by at least 3% relative to vanilla models on at least 2 of 3 tested architectures (DeiT-S, DeiT-B, Swin-T)
Clean accuracy degradation is less than 0.5%
Spectral analysis clearly reveals distinct frequency signatures per corruption type in token embedding space
secondary: STG shows complementary gains when combined with training-time robust methods (FAN, AugMax)
Generalizes across both columnar (DeiT) and hierarchical (Swin) ViT architectures
Gating visualizations qualitatively confirm correct identification of corrupted regions
refutation: Spectral signatures of corruptions are not reliably separable from clean distributions in embedding space
Gating corrupted tokens hurts accuracy because model relies on corrupted features for classification
Clean accuracy drops by more than 1%
